question_clean: Should more or less decision-making for protecting the environment and reducing climate change occur at the European level? (M)
question_en: QB4.5 For each of the following areas, please indicate if you believe that more decision-making should take place at European level or on the contrary, that less decision-making should take place at European level. :- Protecting the environment and reducing climate change (M)
summary:
All EU countries must reduce emissions by 2030 compared to 2005
Reduction targets for each member state based on GDP per capita and cost-effectiveness
Possibilities to trade, borrow and save emissions will be limited
More transparency as information on national actions to be made public
summary_clean: The European Parliament voted to approve a plan requiring all EU countries to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 compared to 2005 levels. The reduction targets for each member state will be based on GDP per capita and cost-effectiveness, with limited possibilities for emissions trading, borrowing, and saving. Additionally, there will be increased transparency, with national actions regarding emissions to be made public.

question_clean: Which products, considering children's need for healthy diets, should be distributed through the EU school scheme? (Multiple answers possible)

summary:
The European Parliament adopted by 534 votes to 57, with 23 abstentions, a resolution on the implementation of the school scheme for fruit, vegetables, milk and dairy products under the Common Market Organisation Regulation.
Members recalled that a healthy lifestyle, including regular physical activity, is an increasingly important issue and should become a priority for society. Promoting a healthy diet is a preventive measure against disease and an investment in public health. One in three children in the EU between the ages of 6 and 9 is overweight or obese. Nutrition education is essential to stimulate healthy eating habits and should therefore be improved across the EU…
summary_clean: The European Parliament passed a resolution, with majority support, to enhance nutrition education for promoting healthy diets as a preventive measure against childhood obesity in the EU.

question_clean: Do you agree or disagree that promoting education for all in developing countries should be one of the EU's main priorities?
question_en: QB6. To what extent do you agree or disagree with the following statement? 'Promoting education for all in developing countries should be one of the main priorities of the EU in developing countries'
summary:
The European Parliament adopted by 495 votes to 27, with 115 abstentions, a resolution on EU development cooperation to enhance access to education and training in developing countries.
Members reaffirmed that everyone has the right to education and that education, notably basic education, must be free, inclusive, accessible without any form of discrimination and compulsory in order to meet the SDGs by 2030, while technical and professional education, as well as higher education, must be equally accessible.
Parliament stressed the key role of education as a vehicle for sharing values, raising awareness and reducing the risk of conflict.
It called on the EU to support cooperation programmes that contribute to educating and raising awareness…
summary_clean: The European Parliament passed a resolution (495-27) to strengthen access to education in developing countries, emphasizing free, inclusive, and compulsory basic education, and equal access to technical, professional, and higher education. The parliament also highlighted education's role in promoting shared values, raising awareness, and reducing conflict.

question_clean: Which topics should be included in the EU school scheme's educational measures, according to your opinion? (Multiple answers possible)
question_en: QA25. In your opinion, which of the following topics should be covered by the educational measures in the EU school scheme?  (MULTIPLE ANSWERS POSSIBLE)
summary:
The European Parliament adopted by 568 votes to 21, with 43 abstentions,  a resolution on ‘Towards an EU strategy to promote education for children in the world: mitigating the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic’.
According to Unicef, more than 168 million children have lost a full year of education because of school closures due to the COVID-19 lockdowns. Education has been significantly disrupted for 800 million students worldwide, who have lost an average of two-thirds of a school year. Since March 2020, some 194 countries have been forced to close schools across their territory due to the COVID-19 pandemic, affecting more than 1.8 billion learners worldwide and depriving them of access to education and other benefits offered by school…
summary_clean: The European Parliament passed a resolution aimed at developing an EU strategy for children's education during the COVID-19 pandemic, addressing the loss of over 168 million children's full year of education due to school closures.

There are still disparities between urban and rural areas in terms of cohesion policy funding, with urban areas receiving three times more funding than rural areas.

The European Parliament adopted by 550 votes to 7, with 4 abstentions, a resolution on the risk of the death penalty and the execution of singer Yahaya Sharif-Aminu for blasphemy in Nigeria.

The European Parliament adopted by 578 votes to 39, with 15 abstentions, a resolution on non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
Members recalled that non-communicable diseases (NCDs) are diseases that are not passed from person to person. They cause 90 % of all deaths in the EU , account for 80 % of the health burden in EU countries and their prevalence continues to grow across the EU. Moreover, premature deaths as a result of cardiovascular diseases (CVDs), cancers, diabetes and chronic respiratory diseases account for 68 % of all premature deaths in Europe. NCDs account for the largest share of Member States’ healthcare expenditures, resulting in an estimated cost of EUR 115 billion, or 0.8 % of GDP annually…
summary_clean: The European Parliament passed a resolution aimed at addressing non-communicable diseases (NCDs), causing 90% of deaths and accounting for 80% of health burden in EU countries. The resolution focuses on reducing premature deaths from CVDs, cancers, diabetes, and chronic respiratory diseases, which account for 68% of all premature deaths in Europe, and aims to control costs estimated at EUR 115 billion annually.
